Diversity of Living and Non-Living Thing.description: Identifying the shared characteristics of living things and classifying them into broad groups like plants, animals, fungi, and bacteria.

Diversity of Materials.description: Exploring the physical properties of various materials and relating them to their specific uses in everyday objects.

Cycles in Plants and Animals (Life Cycle.description: Comparing the different stages of growth and development that various plants and animals pass through during their lives.

Interaction of Forces (Magnets).description: Learning about the properties of magnets, their interactions with magnetic materials, and how they exert push and pull forces.

Plant System (Plant Parts and Functions).description: Identifying the main parts of a plant and understanding the specific role each part plays in the plant's survival.

Human System (Digestive System).description: Studying the organs of the human digestive system and how they work together to break down food for the body to use.

Cycles in Matter and Water (Matter).description: Investigating the properties of matter, its definition as having mass and volume, and the differences between solids, liquids, and gases.

Energy Forms and Uses (Light).description: Understanding how light travels, how shadows are formed, and the way light allows us to see objects.

Energy Forms and Uses (Heat).description: Exploring heat as a form of energy that flows from hotter to colder regions and measuring temperature using various instruments.

Cycles in Plants and Animals (Reproducti.description: Examining how plants and humans reproduce to ensure the continuity of their kind and pass on traits to offspring.

Cycles in Matter and Water (Water).description: Learning about the interchangeable states of water, the processes of the water cycle, and the importance of water conservation.

Human System (Respiratory and Circulator.description: Understanding how the human body takes in oxygen, removes carbon dioxide, and transports nutrients through the blood.

Plant System (Respiratory and Circulator.description: Investigating how food and water are transported throughout a plant via a system of specialised tubes.

Electrical System.description: Building and exploring electric circuits to understand how electricity flows and how different components affect the current.

Energy Forms and Uses (Photosynthesis).description: Studying how plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to produce their own food through photosynthesis.

Energy Conversion.description: Exploring the various forms of energy and understanding how energy is converted from one form to another to do work.

Interaction of Forces.description: Identifying different types of forces, such as gravity and friction, and investigating their effects on the motion and shape of objects.

Interactions within the Environment.description: Investigating the relationships between living things and their environment, including food chains, habitats, and the impact of human activities.
